data.text: Reading the plan file and FEAT-014 spec to understand what needs to be updated.

data.output:
1	# Artifact Generalization & Three-Layer Run Architecture
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-29
4	Status: Approved (refined through 3 review rounds with codex + fresh-eyes)
5	
6	## Summary
7	
8	Two threads converge in this plan:
9	
10	1. **Generalize artifacts beyond markdown documents.** Diagrams, wireframes, hero images, prompts ŌĆö text and binary ŌĆö become first-class artifacts via media-type plurality and sidecar `.ddx.yaml` files. Generated artifacts are checked into git; regeneration is source-hash-driven.
11	2. **Three-layer run architecture.** DDx owns three explicit layered primitives: `ddx run` (single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (bead attempt in isolated worktree), `ddx work` (queue drain). The current two-class storage split (`.ddx/exec-runs/` vs `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/`) collapses into one substrate.
12	
13	These threads converge: **generating an artifact is a layer-1 agent run** with `produces_artifact` metadata.
14	
15	## Decisions (resolved through review)
16	
17	- **Artifact authority:** identity present ŌåÆ artifact. No "managed asset" second-class concept.
18	- **FEAT-019 fate:** child of FEAT-010 (not peer). Contracts to evaluation UX; workflow shapes (comparison, replay, benchmark) move to skills library.
19	- **Server posture:** 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP for all DDx state, sequenced by impact. Writes case-by-case as workflows demand. Only `artifactRegenerate` write surface added in this plan.
20	- **Generated artifacts:** committed to git unconditionally. Size guard (50MB warn / 100MB hard error) with actionable git-lfs guidance; no auto-init / auto-`.gitattributes`.
21	- **CONTRACT-003:** amendments allowed if FEAT-006 work surfaces gaps. The mountable Cobra root export (below) is a load-bearing audit item.
22	- **Run substrate:** single on-disk shape; layer-2 and layer-3 add metadata. `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` collapse.
23	- **No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.** Comparison, replay, benchmark, etc. are skill compositions, never enshrined in Go core or specs.
24	- **Loop ownership sharpened:** DDx owns mechanical queue drain (`ddx work`); content-aware supervisory decisions (e.g., "comparison failed ŌåÆ enqueue reconciliation beads") remain plugin/HELIX territory.
25	- **CLI verbs:** `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` promoted to top-level. Hard-deprecate `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop` (exit non-zero with bare redirect message ŌĆö no aliases, no grace period).
26	- **Generate-artifact layer placement:** pure-return generators (agent returns bytes/text, DDx writes file) are layer-1. Repo-editing generators are layer-2 with bead-less worktree.
27	- **Quorum dispatch:** moves to skill composition. `--quorum` flag goes away; replaced by `compare-prompts` skill.
28	- **`ddx agent` passthrough is structural:** DDx imports the upstream agent module's Cobra root and mounts it under `ddx agent`. Notionally `cli(ddx).agent(load_cli(agent))`. DDx does not enumerate per-subcommand wrappers; the entire upstream tree is the source of truth.
29	
30	## Three-layer architecture
31	
32	| Layer | CLI | Inputs | Outputs / side effects | Owns |
33	|---|---|---|---|---|
34	| 1 | `ddx run` | prompt + agent config | structured output, side effects, run metadata (tokens, model, duration, exit) | invocation atom; consumes upstream `ddx-agent` per CONTRACT-003 |
35	| 2 | `ddx try <bead>` | bead id, base revision | new worktree state + agent-run evidence; merge or preserve | worktree start/end capture; bead ŌåÆ prompt resolution; side-effect bundling |
36	| 3 | `ddx work` | bead queue, stop conditions | sequence of `ddx try` records + loop-level record (drained / blocked / deferred) | mechanical queue drain; no-progress detection |
37	
38	`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`. `ddx work` iterates `ddx try`. One on-disk substrate; layer metadata distinguishes records.
39	
40	`ddx artifact regenerate <id>` is sugar over layer 1 (or layer 2 if the generator edits the repo) with `produces_artifact: <id>` metadata.
41	
42	## Frame artifact updates
43	
44	### docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md
45	- Thesis: "documents AI agents consume" ŌåÆ "artifacts agents produce and consume."
46	- Artifact-management bullet: non-document media + generators.
47	- One sentence on producer/consumer space (no separate matrix).
48	- New Design-Philosophy subsection: **Three-layer run architecture** ŌĆö `run` / `try` / `work` named as DDx's loop ownership.
49	- KVPs: "Multi-media artifact graph"; "Three-layer run architecture (run/try/work)"; note invocation is upstream.
50	- Principle #1: "Artifacts are the product (documents primary)."
51	
52	### docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
53	- Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate.
54	- Problem: add "no provenance for generated artifacts."
55	- Goals: reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add "100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP."
56	- Non-goals: sharpen loop wording (mechanical drain in scope; supervisory content-aware decisions out). Add: "DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers."
57	- FEAT-001/005/006/007/008/010/019/021 blurbs updated.
58	
59	### FEAT-001-cli.md
60	- Promote `run` / `try` / `work` to top-level.
61	- `ddx agent` mounts upstream Cobra root structurally; no DDx-defined leaf subcommands beneath it.
62	- Hard-deprecation handlers for `ddx agent {run, execute-bead, execute-loop}`.
63	- New namespaces: `ddx runs` (cross-layer evidence introspection), `ddx tries` (layer-2 specifically), `ddx work workers` (layer-3 worker management).
64	- Remove FEAT-001:92 backward-compatibility clause for `ddx agent execute-loop`.
65	
66	### FEAT-005-artifacts.md
67	- Identity broadens to non-markdown via sidecar `.ddx.yaml`.
68	- Add `media_type` and `generated_by` fields.
69	- Authority rule documented: identity present ŌåÆ artifact.
70	- Terminology cleanup ("identity," not "ddx: identity").
71	
72	### FEAT-006-agent-service.md
73	- Tighten to layer-1 consumer-side wrapper that powers `ddx run`.
74	- `ddx agent` is structural passthrough (mount upstream Cobra root).
75	- Non-bead Profile/Permissions selection (artifact-keyed path).
76	- Migration table: what stays in FEAT-006 (CONTRACT-003 boundary, profile/permissions), what moves to FEAT-010 (worktree, merge/preserve, evidence bundles, queue drain), what moves to FEAT-001 (CLI surface).
77	- Session-log boundary clarified: DDx owns the envelope/pointer in evidence bundles; upstream owns inner log shape.
78	
79	### FEAT-010-executions.md (substantial refactor)
80	- Three-layer architecture explicit and load-bearing.
81	- Substrate unification: one record shape; layer metadata.
82	- Migrate `.ddx/exec-runs/` and `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` to one layout.
83	- Specify `ddx work` no-progress / stop conditions.
84	- Narrow read-only amendment for `artifactRegenerate` only.
85	- No run-type catalog beyond the three layers.
86	
87	### FEAT-007-doc-graph.md
88	- Sidecar scanner refactor; `media_type`; `generated_by` edge with separate staleness rule (provenance, not authority ŌĆö does not cascade like `depends_on`); version bump on Complete-status feature.
89	- 100% read endpoints for graph state, generated-artifact metadata, sidecar content.
90	
91	### FEAT-008-web-ui.md
92	- Media-type-aware rendering (markdown, mermaid SVG, excalidraw embed, image inline, PDF embed).
93	- Regenerate action wired to `artifactRegenerate` mutation; reconcile with existing FEAT-008:391 weak Re-run requirement.
94	- Layer-aware run views (`work` ŌåÆ `try` ŌåÆ `run` drill-down).
95	
96	### FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md
97	- Contract to evaluation UX (comparison views, grading rubric storage and display, benchmark result aggregation).
98	- Workflow shapes ŌåÆ skills library.
99	- Remove peer-to-FEAT-010 assertions (FEAT-019:40, :404).
100	
101	### FEAT-021-dashboard-ui.md
102	- Layer-aware run-history routes against unified substrate.
103	
104	### FEAT-014, FEAT-013
105	- One-sentence touches: cost attribution and concurrency for generate-artifact.
106	
107	## `ddx agent` subcommand fates
108	
109	`ddx agent` becomes a structural mount of the upstream agent CLI Cobra root. Per-file fate in DDx:
110	
111	**Delete with prejudice (upstream owns these via mounted subtree):**
112	- `agent_list.go`
113	- `agent_capabilities.go`
114	- `agent_doctor.go`
115	- `agent_check.go`
116	- `agent_models.go`
117	- `agent_providers.go`
118	- `agent_route_status.go`
119	- `agent_catalog.go`
120	- `agent_reindex.go`
121	- `agent_benchmark.go` (replaced by `benchmark-suite` skill)
122	- `agent_replay.go` (replaced by `replay-bead` skill)
123	
124	**Move to other DDx namespaces (DDx-only concerns):**
125	- `agent_executions.go` ŌåÆ `tries_*.go`
126	- `agent_log.go` ŌåÆ `runs_log.go`
127	- `agent_metrics.go` ŌåÆ `runs_metrics.go`
128	- `agent_usage.go` ŌåÆ folded into `runs_metrics.go`
129	- `agent_workers.go` ŌåÆ `work_workers.go`
130	
131	**DDx-side overrides on the mounted subtree:**
132	- Deprecation handlers for `agent run` / `agent execute-bead` / `agent execute-loop`.
133	- `agent condense` (DDx-specific output filter ŌĆö keep as override or move upstream during impl pass).
134	
135	**`agent_cmd.go` itself thins to:** mount upstream Cobra root + register DDx overrides.
136	
137	## Skill library (DDx-shipped workflows)
138	
139	Per FEAT-011's path model: source at `skills/ddx/`, embedded at `cli/internal/skills/ddx/`, init-time copies to `.claude/skills/ddx/`, `.agents/skills/ddx/`, `.ddx/skills/ddx/`.
140	
141	- `compare-prompts` ŌĆö N-arm dispatch + aggregation (replaces `--quorum` flag and `agent benchmark`)
142	- `replay-bead` ŌĆö re-run with altered conditions, baseline diff (replaces `agent replay`)
143	- `benchmark-suite` ŌĆö compare across prompt matrix
144	- `adversarial-review`
145	- `effort-estimate`
146	- `bead-breakdown`
147	
148	## Bead breakdown
149	
150	**Frame updates (sequential where dependencies require):**
151	
152	1. Update product-vision.md (+ three-layer subsection)
153	2. Update prd.md (+ three-layer goal, sharpened loop non-goal, no-catalog non-goal)
154	3. Update FEAT-001 (CLI surface refactor; remove backcompat clause; new namespaces) ŌĆö **hard predecessor for #6, #7**
155	4. Update FEAT-005 (multi-media identity refactor)
156	5. CONTRACT-003 audit ŌĆö confirm `ddx-agent` exports a mountable Cobra root and other gaps
157	6. *(Conditional)* Open CONTRACT-003 amendment in `~/Projects/agent` if #5 surfaces gaps
158	7. Update FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer; structural passthrough; non-bead Profile selection; migration table; session-log envelope boundary)
159	8. Refactor FEAT-010 (three-layer architecture; substrate unification; on-disk migration; no-progress stop conditions; narrow `artifactRegenerate` amendment)
160	9. Update FEAT-007 (sidecar refactor; media_type; generated_by; separate staleness; version bump)
161	10. Contract FEAT-019 to evaluation UX
162	11. Update FEAT-008 (media rendering, regenerate action, layer-aware run views)
163	12. Update FEAT-021 (layer-aware run-history routes)
164	13. Update FEAT-014 (cost attribution for generate-artifact)
165	14. Update FEAT-013 (concurrency note for regenerate)
166	15. **Repo-wide audit & verb migration** ŌĆö atomic with #3:
167	    - Delete DDx reimplementations of upstream subcommands (list above).
168	    - Migrate DDx-only run/evidence subcommands to `runs`/`tries`/`work workers`.
169	    - Register DDx overrides on the mounted subtree (deprecations + condense).
170	    - Delete `agent_reindex.go` outright.
171	    - Update scripts/hooks/library skills/personas/docs/README/demos to new verbs.
172	
173	**Design artifacts (concurrent with frame):**
174	
175	16. Architecture: artifact identity schema (concurrent with #4)
176	17. Architecture: graph schema with media_type + generated_by + separate staleness (concurrent with #9)
177	18. Architecture: three-layer run substrate ŌĆö record shape, layer metadata, on-disk layout, migration, `ddx work` stop conditions (concurrent with #8)
178	19. Sidecar `.ddx.yaml` schema design
179	
180	**Read-coverage thread:**
181	
182	20. Read-coverage audit ŌĆö enumerate every CLI-visible read; map to HTTP/MCP coverage; identify gaps
183	21. Per-gap beads ŌĆö sequenced by user-visibility impact, land case-by-case (this is a pointer to ongoing work, not a single bead)
184	
185	**Skill library:**
186	
187	22. `compare-prompts` skill
188	23. `replay-bead` skill
189	24. `benchmark-suite` skill
190	25. `adversarial-review` skill
191	26. `effort-estimate` skill
192	27. `bead-breakdown` skill
193	
194	**Implementation (P2, after frame & design):**
195	
196	- CLI: `ddx run` / `ddx try` top-level commands; mount upstream agent Cobra root; deprecation handlers
197	- CLI: `ddx runs` / `ddx tries` / `ddx work workers` namespaces (subsuming migrated subcommands)
198	- CLI: `ddx artifact regenerate <id>`, `ddx artifact list --media-type <t>`
199	- Storage: sidecar reader/writer; size-guard threshold/warning/tests
200	- Graph: `generated_by` edges + provenance staleness rule
201	- Run framework: refactor to one substrate per #18; generator metadata; `ddx work` no-progress detection
202	- Server: `artifactRegenerate` write endpoint
203	- Server UI: media-type renderers; regenerate action; layer-aware run history
204	- Migration: passive `media_type` only (read-time inference from extension); on-disk run-record migration per #18
205	
206	## Constraints
207	
208	- Beads #3, #7, #8, #15 must merge atomically ŌĆö old verbs are referenced in scripts/library/personas/init help text/CI; an isolated #3 break the in-flight `ddx-refine-plan` skill that produced this plan.
209	- Bead #5 (CONTRACT-003 audit) gates #7 (FEAT-006). #6 conditional.
210	- Architecture sketches (#16ŌĆō#19) run concurrent with their corresponding frame beads, not after ŌĆö keeps frame/design from drifting.
211	- Read-coverage epic (#20ŌĆō#21) is independent and not gated on this plan.
212	
213	## Refinement provenance
214	
215	Refined through 3 rounds of adversarial review (codex via `ddx agent run --harness codex` + fresh-eyes general-purpose subagent in parallel) plus user-resolved questions Q1ŌĆōQ12. Key user decisions: artifact authority (Q1), FEAT-019 child-of-010 (Q2), reads-matter-writes-case-by-case (Q3), check-in unconditionally (Q4), CONTRACT-003 amendments allowed (Q5), substrate unification (Q6), no run-type catalog (Q7), no v1-gate framing (Q8), FEAT-019 contracts (Q9), generate-artifact split layer-1/layer-2 (Q10), quorum-as-skill (Q11), structural passthrough not per-command (Q12 follow-up).
216

data.text: Reading FEAT-014 to understand what needs to be updated.

data.output:
1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: FEAT-014
4	  depends_on:
5	    - helix.prd
6	    - FEAT-006
7	    - FEAT-002
8	    - FEAT-008
9	---
10	# Feature: Agent Usage Awareness and Routing Signals
11	
12	**ID:** FEAT-014
13	**Status:** In Progress
14	**Priority:** P1
15	**Owner:** DDx Team
16	
17	## Overview
18	
19	DDx's agent wrapper should understand provider-native usage and routing signals
20	across harnesses so that every `ddx agent run` can make an informed decision
21	about which harness to use based on request fit, availability, cost,
22	performance, and current quota/headroom when known.
23	
24	## Problem Statement
25	
26	**Current situation:**
27	- DDx can extract per-invocation token and cost data from subprocess output for
28	  codex, claude, and opencode.
29	- DDx currently aggregates usage through its own session-log model, but that
30	  duplicates external provider storage and suppresses native persistence for
31	  codex and claude by default.
32	- Codex native session JSONL already carries `token_count` and `rate_limits`
33	  data when persistence is enabled, which is more useful for routing than DDx's
34	  current duplicate session ledger.
35	- Claude exposes historical usage via `~/.claude/stats-cache.json`, but the
36	  best current-quota source is still unresolved. DDx should exhaust stable
37	  non-PTY options before accepting PTY automation.
38	- The embedded `ddx-agent` runtime is gaining its own session logging and OTEL
39	  telemetry. DDx should consume that telemetry minimally rather than re-owning
40	  the runtime's logs.
41	- DDx does not yet have a normalized routing-signal model that combines
42	  capability, availability, quota/headroom, cost, and DDx-observed performance
43	  into one preflight harness decision.
44	
45	**Desired outcome:** Every `ddx agent run` evaluates candidate harnesses using
46	normalized routing signals:
47	- request fit (profile/model/effort/permissions)
48	- provider availability and authentication
49	- current quota/headroom where known
50	- cost estimate or cost class
51	- DDx-observed performance and reliability
52	
53	External harnesses preserve native persistence. DDx owns only the minimal
54	activity and performance metadata needed for routing and provenance.
55	
56	## Spike Findings
57	
58	### Per-Invocation Data (implemented)
59	
60	| Harness | Token Data | Cost Data | Status |
61	|---------|-----------|-----------|--------|
62	| codex | `turn.completed` JSONL: `input_tokens`, `cached_input_tokens`, `output_tokens` | Not reported | Working |
63	| claude | JSON envelope: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cache_read/creation_input_tokens` | `total_cost_usd` | Working |
64	| opencode | JSON envelope: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens` (if present) | `total_cost_usd` (if present) | Working |
65	| gemini | Untested; no live auth available | Unknown | Blocked |
66	
67	### Signal-Source Matrix (re-reviewed)
68	
69	| Source family | Current quota/headroom | Historical usage | Freshness | Scope | Stable enough for preflight routing? | Notes |
70	|--------------|------------------------|------------------|-----------|-------|-------------------------------------|-------|
71	| Codex native session JSONL / local state | Yes, when persistence is enabled and `token_count.rate_limits` is present | Yes, recent usage totals from session JSONL and local state | Near-real-time once Codex writes the session log; stale if the file is missing or unreadable | Machine-local, authenticated Codex session/account state | Yes, preferred live source for Codex routing when readable | Treat missing persistence or unreadable logs as `unknown`; do not rely on inline PTY scraping in the routing path |
72	| Claude `~/.claude/stats-cache.json` | No stable non-PTY source confirmed yet; surface as `unknown` | Yes, account-wide daily activity, daily tokens by model, cumulative model usage, session counts, and hour-of-day distribution | Cached and delayed; freshness depends on the last stats-cache write | Machine-local cache with account-wide historical usage | Yes for historical usage, no for current quota | Use this source for history and load balancing only; current quota remains `unknown` until a stable live source is confirmed |
73	| Claude runtime / statusline / SDK / hook artifacts | None confirmed | None confirmed | N/A unless a trustworthy source is discovered | N/A until validated | No, not yet | Investigate these before any PTY fallback; if live probing becomes necessary, feed an async snapshot cache rather than routing-time terminal scraping |
74	| embedded `ddx-agent` telemetry | Not a provider quota source | Yes, DDx-owned invocation activity, runtime metrics, and session references | Per invocation or per write | Local workspace / install / session | Yes for DDx-observed performance and provenance; no for provider quota routing | Consume references and derived metrics only; do not duplicate provider transcript storage or provider quota state |
75	
76	Interpretation:
77	
78	- `unknown` means no trustworthy live source exists, the source is stale, or the data is unreadable.
79	- Unknown quota/headroom stays viable for routing with reduced confidence; it is not fabricated into `ok` or `blocked`.
80	- If a current quota source requires active probing, the probe must populate an async snapshot history and never block routing on synchronous PTY scraping.
81	
82	### Research Priorities
83	
84	1. **Codex adapter from native logs** ŌĆö use native session JSONL as the first
85	   source of truth for current headroom and recent totals.
86	2. **Claude non-PTY quota research** ŌĆö investigate statusline, SDK, hook, or
87	   other local runtime sources before accepting any PTY-based solution.
88	3. **Minimal DDx-owned routing metrics** ŌĆö define the smallest DDx state needed
89	   to compare performance and reliability across harnesses without duplicating
90	   provider transcripts.
91	4. **Snapshot history and subscription-cost proxy** ŌĆö when a current quota
92	   source requires active probing, DDx should checkpoint quota snapshots over
93	   time, map them to native usage accumulation, and estimate subscription burn
94	   pressure well enough to balance providers intelligently.
95	
96	## Requirements
97	
98	### Functional
99	
100	**Per-invocation extraction (complete)**
101	1. codex harness: `--json` flag, `turn.completed` JSONL parsing
102	2. claude harness: `--output-format=json`, JSON envelope parsing with
103	   `total_cost_usd`
104	3. opencode harness: `run --format json`, JSON envelope parsing
105	4. `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cost_usd` fields on DDx invocation
106	   activity rows
107	5. `ddx agent usage` command with harness/time-window/machine-readable output
108	6. gemini harness: investigate `--output-format=json` output when auth is
109	   available; implement token extraction if format is known
110	
111	**Provider-native signal ingestion**
112	7. **Native persistence preserved** ŌĆö DDx must not pass suppressive flags such
113	   as codex `--ephemeral` or claude `--no-session-persistence` by default for
114	   external harnesses.
115	8. **Codex native signal adapter** ŌĆö read current quota/headroom and recent
116	   usage from native Codex session JSONL when persistence is enabled.
117	9. **Claude historical usage adapter** ŌĆö read `~/.claude/stats-cache.json` to
118	   obtain account-wide daily token consumption by model, session counts, and
119	   activity patterns.
120	10. **Claude current quota spike** ŌĆö identify a stable non-PTY current-quota
121	    source if one exists. If no stable source is found, DDx must surface quota
122	    as `unknown` rather than inventing it. PTY automation is explicitly a
123	    fallback of last resort, not the MVP path, and must feed an asynchronous
124	    snapshot/sampling path rather than synchronous routing-time scraping.
125	11. **Signal freshness/cache policy** ŌĆö cache provider-native signal reads with
126	    explicit freshness semantics so `ddx agent run` can distinguish fresh from
127	    stale state.
128	
129	**Normalized routing signals**
130	12. **Routing signal model** ŌĆö DDx normalizes every candidate's:
131	    - capability/request fit
132	    - availability/authentication state
133	    - quota/headroom state (`ok`, `blocked`, `unknown`)
134	    - cost estimate or cost class
135	    - DDx-observed performance metrics
136	    - freshness / confidence
137	13. **Minimal DDx-owned metrics** ŌĆö DDx stores only compact outcome samples,
138	    async quota snapshots, and derived burn summaries. Those records are
139	    attributed to the resolved canonical target or exact model pin so
140	    different models on the same surface do not share one routing signal.
141	    Outcome samples may record recent success/failure, latency, and last
142	    observed token/cost values when available; DDx does not store provider
143	    transcripts or native session bodies as routing inputs.
144	14. **Snapshot history for live quota sources** ŌĆö when DDx depends on an
145	    actively probed quota source, it checkpoints time-stamped quota snapshots
146	    asynchronously and relates them to native usage accumulation to build a
147	    history of usage plus quota availability.
148	15. **Subscription-cost proxy** ŌĆö DDx derives a relative subscription burn
149	    estimate from quota snapshots and usage deltas well enough to balance
150	    providers under subscription plans where direct per-token billing is
151	    unavailable.
152	16. **Routing integration** ŌĆö harness selection uses the normalized signal
153	    model together with requested profile, model, effort, permission mode, and
154	    explicit harness override semantics.
155	17. **Operator visibility** ŌĆö `ddx agent doctor --routing` and `ddx agent
156	    usage` consume the normalized signal model and report source freshness.
157	
158	**Budgeting and throttling**
159	18. **Deferred scope** ŌĆö budget passthrough, automatic throttling, and pacing
160	    policy remain follow-on work after the signal-source spikes establish the
161	    right acquisition model.
162	
163	**Always-on execute-bead runtime metrics**
164	19. `ddx agent execute-bead` must capture built-in runtime metrics for every
165	    iteration, independent of any graph-authored execution documents. These are
166	    DDx runtime facts, not substitutes for project-authored metric docs. The
167	    fields below are persisted into the project's
168	    `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` attempt bundle (FEAT-006) so the host+user
169	    `ddx-server` and its dashboards can replay runtime metrics from the same
170	    replay-backed artifacts that back execution history ŌĆö DDx does not keep a
171	    separate runtime-metrics store. When execute-bead runs inside the server's
172	    supervised `WorkerManager` (FEAT-002), the worker writes the same bundle to
173	    the project that owns the bead.
174	    Minimum fields (where available):
175	    - `harness`, `model`, `session_id`
176	    - `elapsed_ms`
177	    - `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens`
178	    - `cost_usd` (`-1` when unknown, `0` for local models)
179	    - `base_rev`
180	    - `result_rev`
181	20. **Tracked metrics surface.** The runtime metrics captured in requirement
182	    19 are persisted in the tracked execute-bead attempt bundle at
183	    `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json` per FEAT-006 ┬¦"Execute-Bead
184	    Evidence Bundle". The runtime `harness`, `model`, and `session_id`
185	    values must also be projected into the canonical Git trailer set on
186	    the iteration commit (`Ddx-Harness`, `Ddx-Model`, and `Ddx-Worker-Id`
187	    when no distinct worker identity is available) per FEAT-006
188	    ┬¦"Canonical Git trailers". The commit trailers and the JSON block in
189	    FEAT-006 ┬¦"Iteration commit summary" must both be projected from
190	    `result.json`, never from ad hoc runtime state.
191	
192	### Non-Functional
193	
194	- **Performance:** provider-native signal reads and DDx metrics lookups should
195	  add only modest preflight overhead. Dispatch-time routing should remain fast
196	  enough to avoid noticeable startup delay.
197	- **Accuracy:** Token counts from harness JSON output are authoritative.
198	  Provider-native stores reflect the provider's own accounting and should be
199	  preferred where available.
200	- **Zero fabrication:** DDx must never fabricate current quota/headroom. When a
201	  source is unavailable or stale, DDx surfaces `unknown`.
202	- **Graceful degradation:** If provider-native stores are missing, unreadable,
203	  or in an unexpected format, DDx falls back to request fit, static cost, and
204	  DDx-observed performance metrics. Never fail an invocation solely because a
205	  signal source could not be read.
206	- **Minimal ownership:** DDx owns only the metadata needed for routing,
207	  provenance, and operator visibility. It must not become a shadow transcript
208	  store for external providers.
209	
210	## Dashboard Read Model
211	
212	The normalized routing signal model (requirement 12) doubles as the read model
213	consumed by the FEAT-008 provider dashboard and the FEAT-002 `/api/providers`
214	endpoints. FEAT-014 governs field semantics, unknown-state rules, and freshness
215	conventions for both the routing code path and the UI read path.
216	
217	### Read-Model Fields
218	
219	The provider summary and provider detail shapes are defined in FEAT-002
220	┬¦"Provider Availability and Utilization". FEAT-014 owns the rules that govern
221	every field:
222	
223	**Availability and auth fields**
224	
225	| Field | Type | Source | Unknown condition |
226	|-------|------|--------|-------------------|
227	| `status` | `available` \| `unavailable` \| `unknown` | harness discovery | harness cannot be found or tested |
228	| `auth_state` | `authenticated` \| `unauthenticated` \| `unknown` | per-harness auth probe | probe fails or is not implemented |
229	| `quota_headroom` | `ok` \| `blocked` \| `unknown` | provider-native or `unknown` | no trustworthy live source exists |
230	
231	**Utilization fields**
232	
233	| Field | Type | Source | Unknown sentinel |
234	|-------|------|--------|-----------------|
235	| `historical_usage.*.input_tokens` | integer | provider-native or DDx-observed | `-1` (JSON) ŌåÆ rendered as `ŌĆö` in UI |
236	| `historical_usage.*.output_tokens` | integer | provider-native or DDx-observed | `-1` |
237	| `historical_usage.*.cost_usd` | float | provider-native | `-1` |
238	| `burn_estimate.daily_token_rate` | float | DDx-derived from usage deltas | `-1` |
239	| `burn_estimate.subscription_burn` | `low` \| `moderate` \| `high` \| `unknown` | DDx-derived | `unknown` when no rate history |
240	| `burn_estimate.confidence` | `high` \| `medium` \| `low` | DDx-derived | reflects staleness of contributing signal |
241	| `routing_signals.performance.success_rate` | float 0ŌĆō1 | DDx-observed | `-1` when sample_count < 3 |
242	
243	**Freshness fields**
244	
245	- `freshness_ts` ŌĆö RFC 3339 timestamp of the oldest contributing signal write.
246	  When no signals exist the field is omitted (not zero-time).
247	- `last_checked_ts` ŌĆö when DDx most recently attempted to read signals for this
248	  harness. May differ from `freshness_ts` when the read attempt found no new data.
249	- `signal_sources` ŌĆö ordered list of source identifiers that contributed to
250	  this snapshot. Defined values: `native-session-jsonl`, `stats-cache`,
251	  `ddx-metrics`, `none`. `none` appears alone when no source contributed.
252	
253	### Source Attribution Labels (for UI display)
254	
255	The UI renders per-field source labels to distinguish certainty levels:
256	
257	| `signal_sources` entry | UI label |
258	|------------------------|----------|
259	| `native-session-jsonl` | provider-reported |
260	| `stats-cache` | provider-reported |
261	| `ddx-metrics` | DDx-estimated |
262	| `none` | unknown |
263	
264	When multiple sources contribute, the field-level label reflects the most
265	authoritative source. Fields absent from all sources carry a `?` badge in the UI
266	and a tooltip drawn from `source_note` in the per-model quota object.
267	
268	### Unknown-State Contract
269	
270	**Zero fabrication.** No field may be coerced from `unknown` / `-1` to `ok` / `0`
271	for display convenience. UI components must treat `-1` as `unknown` and render
272	`ŌĆö`, never `0`. Badge components must treat the string literal `"unknown"` as a
273	distinct state with its own visual treatment, never as a fallback for `"ok"`.
274	
275	**Tooltips.** Every `?` badge and every "unknown" pill in the provider dashboard
276	must include a tooltip populated from one of the defined tooltip strings below:
277	
278	| Condition | Tooltip text |
279	|-----------|-------------|
280	| Claude quota unknown | "No stable non-PTY quota source confirmed for Claude. DDx will route with reduced confidence rather than reject." |
281	| Signal source not readable | "The signal source could not be read at the last check. DDx is using cached state." |
282	| No signal history | "No signal history available. DDx will route based on request fit only." |
283	| Stale signal | "Last signal observed more than 1 hour ago. Data may not reflect current provider state." |
284	
285	**Routing behavior.** `quota_headroom: unknown` does not block routing. It
286	reduces routing confidence. `quota_headroom: blocked` rejects the candidate. The
287	dashboard must not imply that `unknown` means `blocked`.
288	
289	### Queryable Read Model
290	
291	The read model supports the following query dimensions for the `/api/providers`
292	list endpoint and for client-side filtering in the provider dashboard:
293	
294	| Dimension | Filter values |
295	|-----------|--------------|
296	| Provider / harness | exact match on `harness` name |
297	| Model | substring or exact match on model identifier |
298	| Status | `available`, `unavailable`, `unknown` |
299	| Auth state | `authenticated`, `unauthenticated`, `unknown` |
300	| Quota state | `ok`, `blocked`, `unknown` |
301	| Signal source | presence of a named source in `signal_sources` |
302	| Time window | scopes `historical_usage` window (7d, 30d) |
303	
304	Sorting is supported on: `harness` (alpha), `status`, `auth_state`,
305	`quota_headroom`, `recent_success_rate`, `recent_latency_p50_ms`,
306	`freshness_ts`. Default sort: `status` ascending, then `harness` ascending.
307	
308	## CLI Interface
309	
310	```bash
311	# Routing state with freshness and quota/headroom where known
312	ddx agent doctor --routing
313	
314	# Usage summary derived from provider-native sources + DDx-owned metrics
315	ddx agent usage
316	
317	# Filter to one harness
318	ddx agent usage --harness claude
319	
320	# Specific time window
321	ddx agent usage --since 7d
322	ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01
323	
324	# Machine-readable
325	ddx agent usage --format json
326	```
327	
328	## User Stories
329	
330	### US-140: Developer Checks Agent Usage and Routing Signals
331	**As a** developer managing agent usage
332	**I want** to see provider-native usage signals and DDx-observed routing metrics
333	**So that** I can understand what DDx is routing on
334	
335	**Acceptance Criteria:**
336	- Given I run `ddx agent usage`, then I see per-harness usage/cost where
337	  available, plus DDx-observed runtime metrics
338	- Given I run `ddx agent usage --since today`, then only today's windows are
339	  counted
340	- Given I run `ddx agent usage --format json`, then output is valid JSON
341	
342	### US-141: DDx Routes Using Current Availability Signals
343	**As** the DDx agent router
344	**I want** to combine request fit, availability, quota/headroom, cost, and
345	performance signals
346	**So that** I choose the best viable harness for each run
347	
348	**Acceptance Criteria:**
349	- Given Codex native session logs are available, when DDx evaluates codex, then
350	  current quota/headroom comes from those native logs rather than PTY
351	  automation
352	- Given Claude has no stable current-quota source yet, when DDx evaluates
353	  claude, then current quota is surfaced as `unknown` rather than fabricated
354	- Given quota is explicitly blocked for a candidate, then that candidate is
355	  rejected
356	- Given quota is unknown for a candidate, then the candidate remains viable but
357	  is ranked with reduced confidence rather than hard-rejected
358	
359	### US-142: Developer Sees Signal Freshness in Doctor Output
360	**As a** developer debugging harness selection
361	**I want** `ddx agent doctor --routing` to show where each signal came from and
362	how fresh it is
363	**So that** I can trust or question DDx's decision with evidence
364	
365	**Acceptance Criteria:**
366	- Given I run `ddx agent doctor --routing`, then each harness reports current
367	  availability state, quota/headroom state, and last-checked freshness
368	- Given a signal came from a provider-native source, then doctor output
369	  identifies that source
370	- Given a signal source could not be read, then doctor output reports `unknown`
371	  with an explanatory note instead of omitting the field
372	
373	### US-143: Operator Views Provider Dashboard in Browser
374	**As an** operator deciding which harnesses to use for a batch of work
375	**I want** to see all configured providers with routing availability, auth state,
376	  quota/headroom, utilization, and burn rate in one browser view
377	**So that** I can make informed dispatch decisions without running CLI commands
378	
379	**Acceptance Criteria:**
380	- Given I open the Provider Dashboard (FEAT-008 ┬¦"Provider / Harness Dashboard"),
381	  then I see one row per configured harness with status, auth, quota/headroom,
382	  cost class, and freshness timestamp
383	- Given a harness has `quota_headroom: unknown`, then the pill renders "unknown"
384	  with a tooltip from the FEAT-014 tooltip registry ŌĆö not a green "ok" badge
385	- Given I click a row, then the detail panel shows the full read-model fields
386	  defined in FEAT-014 ┬¦"Read-Model Fields", with source labels distinguishing
387	  provider-reported from DDx-estimated values
388	- Given a numeric field has no trustworthy source (sentinel `-1`), then the UI
389	  renders `ŌĆö`, not `0`
390	- Given I look at the burn estimate row, then I see `confidence` labeled as
391	  `high`, `medium`, or `low` ŌĆö not omitted
392	- Given I search or filter the provider list, then results update client-side
393	  within 100 ms without a server round-trip
394	
395	### US-144: Operator Distinguishes Provider-Reported vs DDx-Estimated Signals
396	**As an** operator auditing routing signal provenance
397	**I want** each signal field in the provider dashboard to be labeled by its source
398	**So that** I can determine how much weight to place on the value
399	
400	**Acceptance Criteria:**
401	- Given a field came from `native-session-jsonl` or `stats-cache`, then it
402	  carries a "provider-reported" label
403	- Given a field was derived from DDx-observed invocation metrics, then it carries
404	  a "DDx-estimated" label
405	- Given the `signal_sources` list includes `none`, then the harness shows no
406	  usage or burn data, only availability and auth state where detectable
407	
408	### US-145: Execute-bead Runtime Metrics Are Captured Automatically
409	**As** a developer reviewing bead execution history
410	**I want** runtime metrics recorded for every execute-bead iteration without
411	manual instrumentation
412	**So that** iterations are comparable and cost is always visible
413	
414	**Acceptance Criteria:**
415	- Given `ddx agent execute-bead` runs with a harness that exposes token and cost
416	  data, when the iteration completes, then the run record contains `harness`,
417	  `model`, `session_id`, `elapsed_ms`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`,
418	  `total_tokens`, and `cost_usd`
419	- Given runtime token and cost data is captured, when the iteration commit
420	  summary is written, then the summary includes harness, model, total tokens,
421	  cost, base revision, and result revision
422	
423	## Dependencies
424	
425	- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) ŌĆö harness registry, invocation activity capture
426	- FEAT-002 (DDx Server) ŌĆö `/api/providers` and `/api/providers/:harness`
427	  expose FEAT-014's routing signal model to UI and MCP consumers
428	- FEAT-008 (Web UI) ŌĆö the provider dashboard view is the browser surface for
429	  FEAT-014's read model; FEAT-014 governs field semantics, unknown-state
430	  rules, and tooltip registry for that view
431	- provider-native local stores such as `~/.codex/` and `~/.claude/`
432	- embedded `ddx-agent` runtime telemetry and session references
433	
434	## Implementation Strategy
435	
436	### Phase 1 ŌĆö Signal-source spikes
437	- Inventory provider-native signal sources and freshness semantics
438	- Define the minimal routing metrics schema and retention rules
439	  (now part of ddx-agent's internal routing per CONTRACT-003-ddx-agent-service)
440	- Resolve whether Claude has a stable non-PTY current-quota source
441	
442	### Phase 2 ŌĆö Preserve native persistence and add adapters
443	- Remove DDx defaults that suppress codex/claude native persistence
444	- Implement Codex native signal adapter
445	- Implement Claude historical usage adapter
446	
447	### Phase 3 ŌĆö Normalize routing signals
448	- Build the shared routing signal model
449	- Feed doctor/usage/routing from that model
450	- Treat unknown quota as unknown, not as blocked or OK
451	- If a live quota source requires active probing, persist async snapshot history
452	  rather than blocking routing on synchronous PTY gymnastics
453	
454	### Phase 4 ŌĆö Follow-on policy work
455	- Budget passthrough
456	- Throttling / pacing
457	- Optional PTY fallback only if non-PTY Claude quota sourcing proves impossible
458	
459	## Out of Scope
460	
461	- Provider billing API integration
462	- Intercepting harness HTTP traffic for real-time rate-limit headers
463	- Gemini token capture (blocked on auth investigation)
464	- Cross-machine usage aggregation (see FEAT-013)
465	- Immediate budget enforcement and adaptive throttling policy
466	- Prompt design and task strategy beyond harness selection
467

data.text: The plan says FEAT-014 needs a "one-sentence touch: generate-artifact runs consume token budgets via FEAT-014 model." I'll add this to the Overview section.

input.old_string:
DDx's agent wrapper should understand provider-native usage and routing signals
across harnesses so that every `ddx agent run` can make an informed decision
about which harness to use based on request fit, availability, cost,
performance, and current quota/headroom when known.
input.new_string:
DDx's agent wrapper should understand provider-native usage and routing signals
across harnesses so that every `ddx agent run` can make an informed decision
about which harness to use based on request fit, availability, cost,
performance, and current quota/headroom when known. `ddx artifact regenerate`
runs are layer-1 agent invocations and consume token budgets via this same
FEAT-014 normalized signal model.

input.command:
git add docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-014-token-awareness.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(FEAT-014): note generate-artifact runs consume token budgets via FEAT-014 model [ddx-1986ee33]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

data.text: Done. Added a two-sentence note to FEAT-014's Overview clarifying that `ddx artifact regenerate` runs are layer-1 agent invocations that consume token budgets via FEAT-014's normalized signal model. Committed as `c9280ec4`.
